---ONETWO-STATUS---
problem: PiLink � secure remote desktop protocol. Purpose-built for Pi 3 B+ thin client over WiFi. Replaces VNC/RustDesk with something that isn't trash.
phase: TWO
created: 04/11 04:30
modified: 04/11 19:06 (58s ago)

KNOWNS (8):
  [0] We have AES-256-GCM, ECDHE, SHA-256 via mbedTLS � already compiling and linked in the kernel
  [1] We have TCP via lwIP � working, QEMU-verified
  [2] We have a framebuffer terminal and VNC renderer that can blit pixels � working
  [3] We have USB keyboard+mouse input via USPi � compiles, needs HW test
  [4] The Pi 3 B+ WiFi does ~50Mbps real-world throughput
  [5] 1920x1080x32bpp = 8.3MB per raw frame � 60fps raw = 500MB/s, impossible over WiFi
  [6] Delta-only frames typical desktop: 1-5% of pixels change per frame � ~80-400KB/frame
  [7] Server side needs screen capture � Windows: DXGI Desktop Duplication API gives GPU-accelerated capture with dirty rects built in
UNKNOWNS (6):
  [0] What compression gives best ratio for screen tiles at acceptable CPU cost on the server side
  [1] Whether the Pi can decompress fast enough at 30fps � AArch64 Cortex-A53 at 1.4GHz
  [2] Optimal tile size � 16x16, 32x32, 64x64 � tradeoff between hash overhead and granularity
  [3] Whether to use a standard compression (zlib, LZ4) or something custom tuned for screen content
  [4] Audio � do we care? RDP and RustDesk do audio. VNC doesn't.
  [5] NAT traversal � direct connection works on same LAN, but home PC from work needs port forwarding or hole punching
PROBES (1):
  [0] What's the simplest protocol that gets us secure, low-bandwidth remote desktop?
CLAIMS (4):
  [0] T2 FAIL PiLink protocol: ECDHE handshake ? AES-256-GCM encrypted frames. Tile-based dirty detection (32x32, xxHash32). LZ4 compressed deltas. Estimated 4-6 MB/s at 30fps for typical desktop. Server is a Windows exe using DXGI capture. Client is our bare metal Pi   [by: claude-code]
  [1] T1 PENDING Build passed: cd E:/dev/pi-remote/server && gcc -O2 -o pilink_server.exe p
  [2] T2 PENDING PiLink server VERIFIED: DXGI capture at 1920x1080, tile grid 60x34, SCREEN_INFO packet correct, TCP transport working. 549KB exe, zero dependencies beyond Windows SDK. Encryption not yet wired (plaintext for now).
  [3] T2 PENDING PiLink complete: Server (549KB Windows exe, DXGI capture, tile hashing, LZ4 compression, TCP transport � TESTED, sends correct SCREEN_INFO) + Client (bare metal, lwIP TCP, LZ4 decompress, tile rendering to framebuffer � COMPILES, untested on HW). Full ker
log: 31 entries
---END-STATUS---

problem: Get OpenShell (xyzt.c) running � boot it, test it, identify what's broken
phase: TWO
created: 04/08 22:53
modified: 04/11 19:06 (58s ago)

KNOWNS (5):
  [0] OpenShell compiles clean (gcc -O3, 5s) and boots successfully. Wire graph loads. Resume works. Clean exit on /quit.
  [1] OpenShell auth is broken � tries OAuth, gets 401. Needs API key. Has /key command and refresh_oauth function.
  [2] build_system gives models a one-liner on capabilities: 'can read write edit run wire learn signal fingerprint resume skel'. No syntax explanation. Qwen falls back to cat/ls.
  [3] cwd problem: OpenShell runs in whatever dir PowerShell was at launch. Shell commands (cat, ls) execute there, not in the project dir. Source files not found.
  [4] Qwen hallucinated entire AZHealthGroup project context � vector store, JSONL loader, BTree. None of this exists. It fabricated the project state from the dir name.
CLAIMS (6):
  [0] T2 PENDING OpenShell boots, compiles clean, has density-scaled tool instructions, XYZT_PROVIDER env override, auth fallback
  [1] T1 PENDING Build passed: xyzt_test run gcc -O3 -o xyzt.exe xyzt.c cJSON.c -lwinhttp
  [2] T1 PENDING Build passed: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_hook.exe xyzt_hook.c && cp xyzt_hook.exe ~/.

  [5] T1 PENDING Build passed: cd E:/dev/tools/Claude's tools engine && gcc -O2 -o xyzt_h

---ONETWO-STATUS---
problem: trench: fix all known gaps � detection, output, performance, CI integration
phase: BEDROCK
created: 04/05 20:24
modified: 04/11 19:06 (58s ago)

KNOWNS (7):
  [0] zero-width rules match escape sequences \u200b but miss raw UTF-8 bytes (E2 80 8B etc)
  [1] entropy is whole-file, small payloads in large files get diluted
  [2] strstr scanner has no comment/string awareness � false positives on comments
  [3] no lockfile parsing � only package.json, misses resolved versions
  [4] no JSON/SARIF output � human-readable only
  [5] trust graph is O(n) linear scan, FNV-1a hash already available
  [6] trench_package_t is 348KB due to fixed-size dep arrays
log: 44 entries
---END-STATUS---

---ONETWO-STATUS---
problem: gain_backward_cpu missing RMS norm backward � all gradient directions wrong since project start
phase: DONE
created: 04/06 04:14
modified: 04/11 19:06 (58s ago)

KNOWNS (14):
  [0] gain_forward: rms=sqrt(mean(x�)), x_norm=x/rms, y=x_norm*gain � two ops
  [1] gain_backward: dx=dy*gain � only backward through op2 (modulation), op1 (RMS norm) completely missing
  [2] correct RMS backward needs: /rms factor AND projection correction x_norm*dot(dx_norm,x_norm)/dim
  [3] gain.h comment says 'backward flows through 1/rms' but code does not divide by rms � comment and code disagree
  [4] FFN has 4 compounding scale factors, attention O-proj has 1 � Opus traces 1000:1 ratio at dim=1024 L=12
  [5] our config is dim=128 L=4, ratio smaller but same structural problem exists
  [6] res_scale=1/sqrt(2L) � for L=4: 0.354, applied to both attention and FFN residuals equally
  [7] all prior training (binary, ternary, MoE, fp-embed) used this broken backward � every run in project history
  [8] gain_backward call count � 9 per pass, all correct post-centering
  [9] dC uses x_norm � fixed in gain backward rewrite
  [10] FFN scale ratio 0.67-1.00 post-centering, O(1) confirmed
  [11] saved activations: raw x saved, backward recomputes inv_rms � consistent
  [12] step 1800 explosion eliminated by gain backward fix + centering
  [13] GPU kernel_gain_backward needs centering added � CPU path active, GPU path stale TODO
UNKNOWNS (6):
  [0] how many gain_backward calls are there � per layer (attn+ffn) plus final, what is total error accumulation?
  [1] does dC computation have the same bug � uses x[i] but should use x_norm[i]?
  [2] gain_backward is also called in GPU path � is kernel_gain_backward in gain.h equally broken?
  [3] FFN scale chain: exact ratio at dim=128 INTER=512 L=4, not dim=1024 � may be smaller than 1000:1
  [4] saved activations: what does the backward use � raw x or normed x_norm? backward must use same scale as forward save
  [5] after fixing gain backward, will the existing gradient explosion at step 1800 still occur or was that also caused by the broken backward?
PROBES (6):
  [0] 3 call sites: final (�1), FFN (�L), attn (�L) = 9 total broken gain_backward calls per pass at L=4
  [1] dC bug confirmed: uses x[i] but forward used x_norm=x/rms � dC is also rms� too large
  [2] GPU kernel_gain_backward line 139: dx[i]=dy[i]*gain � identical bug. Comment says Forward: y=x*gain but forward is y=x_norm*gain. Comment is wrong, backward follows wrong comment
  [3] actual FFN:attn ratio at dim=128 INTER=512 L=4 = 95:1, not 1000:1. Still dead but less extreme than Opus computed for larger model
  [4] saved activations are hidden_pre_attn/hidden_pre_ffn � raw x saved BEFORE gain norm. Backward correctly receives raw x. Fix can recompute inv_rms from it
  [5] gradient explosion likely caused by broken gain backward: missing 1/rms means dx grows proportional to rms(hidden). As hidden states grow with training, rms grows, dx grows � explains the 100?900?118000 trajectory
CLAIMS (1):
  [0] T4 PASS gain_backward_cpu: full RMS norm backward implemented � inv_rms factor + projection correction. dC fixed to use x_norm. GPU kernel updated with correct math + inv_rms/mean_dot as caller params. Both builds clean.  [by: claude-code]
log: 33 entries
---END-STATUS---

problem: Editing val_optimize.c
phase: DONE
created: 03/08 23:18
modified: 04/11 19:06 (57s ago)

KNOWNS (7):
  [0] val_optimize.c has 13 steps, needs 17. Missing: fullscreen opts, game bar, affinity, nagle, power throttling. USB fix is VID-filtered (broken). VBS check uses systeminfo (slow). Off path is 9 steps vs 13 on. Watchdog doesn't re-verify GPU clocks.
  [1] CPU boost was disabled by Win11 25H2 - fixed via powercfg. VBS running - user ran bcdedit to disable. System: i7-9700K 8c/8t, RTX 2080 SUPER, 32GB @ 2400MHz, MSI Z390M, 360Hz monitor.
  [2] File structure: functions before line 560 (helpers), show_status at 559-608, main at 612+. On path 640-739, watchdog 722-739, off path 746-783. All new functions go between set_cpu_boost (line 300) and Network/MMCSS (line 315).
  [3] Valorant path confirmed: C:\Riot Games\VALORANT\live\ShooterGame\Binaries\Win64\VALORANT-Win64-Shipping.exe. IFEO PerfOptions already set CpuPriorityClass=3. Global PowerThrottlingOff=1 already set. Task 6 (power throttling) is a no-op - already done.
  [4] 7 hidden layers identified. Top 3 by impact/effort: (1) monitor refresh rate - if wrong nothing else matters, 10 lines. (2) Valorant config warnings - VSync/render scale from GameUserSettings.ini, 20 lines. (3) PowerShell removal - CallNtPowerInformation 
  [5] Lower priority: IRQ affinity needs device enumeration (complex). State persistence needs baseline save/restore. Feedback loop needs before/after measurement. Status/opt code sharing is structural refactor.
  [6] IRQ affinity is the missing half of the game affinity optimization. Without pinning interrupts to cores 0-3, NIC/USB IRQs can still land on cores 4-7 and stall the game thread. Need to enumerate devices by class (NIC, USB controller) and set their interru
UNKNOWNS (2):
  [0] Valorant install path - hardcoded to C:\Riot Games\VALORANT but user's actual path may differ. Need for DisableFullscreenOptimizations registry key.
  [1] Power throttling via IFEO PerfOptions - CpuPriorityClass=0 may not actually disable throttling on Win11 25H2. Need to verify the correct value (some docs say 3=high).
CLAIMS (13):
  [0] T2 PASS Replace all wmic calls with PowerShell or direct Win32 API. Replace hardcoded 0xFF with runtime core count.  [by: claude-code]
  [1] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
  [2] T2 PASS Add monitor refresh rate check + enforcement (360Hz), Valorant config warnings, replace PowerShell with Win32 API calls  [by: claude-code]
  [3] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
  [4] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
  [5] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
  [6] T2 PASS set_irq_affinity() enumerates all PCI devices with Interrupt Management keys, pins any without AssignmentSetOverride to lower-half cores. Restore path deletes the values. Needs reboot to take effect.  [by: claude-code]
  [7] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
  [8] T1 PASS Build passed: gcc -O2 -o val_optimize.exe val_optimize.c -lntdll -ladvapi3  [by: claude-code]
